record.text:
Into the Wind and Vivarium stood out to me for different reasons.

One feels adventurous and full of movement.
The other creates atmosphere and quiet curiosity.

Games with identity often stay longer than the biggest reveals.

#AnimeGaming #GamingCulture #CozyGames#AnimeGaming #AnimeCommunity

images.alt:
⬆️ Into the Wind and ⬇️ Vivarium

Two showcase reveals caught my attention for different reasons:

✈️ Into the Wind
The idea of combining air pirates, deliveries and adventure creates a sense of movement and exploration that feels refreshing.

🌿 Vivarium
A very different experience — quieter, atmosphere-driven and visually expressive. A reminder that memorable player experiences don’t always come from scale.

As someone interested in player experience, multilingual communities and localization, I enjoy seeing projects that build identity through feeling, worldbuilding and atmosphere.

Not every game needs to be the loudest announcement to leave an impression.

#Gaming #AnimeGaming #PlayerExperience #GamingCulture #AnimeCommunity #CozyGames #GameIndustry

📣 New Podcast! "What Does Philosophy Have to Do with AI?" on @Spreaker #ai #bc #canada #education #ethics #generativeai #k12 #leadership #philosophy #superintendent #teaching #technology #virtues

external.description:
Dean interviews high school philosophy teacher Marcus Blair about how discovering a student had used ChatGPT shifted his initial curiosity into fear and urgency, prompting questions about AI’s impact on classrooms, education, and society. Blair explains how his philosophy background helps him ground AI decisions in critical thinking and ethics, drawing on thinkers like Descartes and approaches like utilitarianism and virtue ethics to move beyond policing toward student integrity. He describes practical classroom systems—AI integration “stamps,” a signed student AI agreement, and QR-code disclosure forms—plus strategic cognitive offloading so students can focus on intended learning outcomes. Blair shares examples including AI-supported reporting followed by in-class ethical judgment, emphasizing standards-based assessment, process-focused writing instruction, reduced homework, and district leadership supporting capacity-building and dialogue.

record.text: 📣 New Podcast! "Do You Just Tweet All Day?" on @Spreaker #brand #canada #communications #education #k12 #ontario #schoolboards #schools #socialmedia

external.description:
Ryan Strang on the Evolution of School Communications: Strategy, Storytelling, Crisis, and Trust Dean interviews Ryan Strang, Senior Manager of Communications and Community Relations for Ontario’s Grand Erie District School Board and president/executive director of CASE, about how school communications has evolved beyond “just tweeting” into strategic advising, storytelling, brand ambassadorship, customer service, and crisis management. Ryan describes how social media shifted communications from one-way to fast, two-way engagement, increasing pressure for rapid response and amplifying challenges like polarization and misinformation. They discuss choosing channels based on metrics and audience (including stepping back from X, focusing on Facebook/Instagram, and monitoring/responding on Reddit), balancing playful brand voice with professionalism, and the emerging opportunities and risks of AI—especially around privacy and student images. Ryan shares a career highlight: quickly organizing a maple-syrup outdoor education experience for Syrian newcomer families in Peel, showing communications’ impact through relationship-building and storytelling.

record.text: 📣 New Podcast! "What Is AI Literacy?" on @Spreaker #ai #artificialintelligence #canada #children #education #k12 #leadership #literacy #lobby

external.description:
Dean interviews Kate Arthur about what it means for a skill to become a “literacy” and why AI literacy must build on foundational reading, writing, and numeracy alongside access to networks, tools, and skills. Arthur shares her nontraditional education path, 25 years at the intersection of tech, communications, and education, and how classroom moments like students using Siri pushed her to connect literacy with AI’s growing presence. They discuss integrating AI learning across subjects rather than isolating it, the risk of focusing on tools over outcomes like critical thinking, and concerns about widening divides when basic literacy gaps persist. Arthur describes a shift from an attention economy to an attachment economy in generative AI, the ethics of human–machine relationships, and the need to democratize AI knowledge currently concentrated among a few leaders. She also recounts lobbying that secured $6M for youth skills education and emphasizes community-driven change.

external.description:
Dean interviews Kate Arthur about what it means for a skill to become a “literacy” and why AI literacy must build on foundational reading, writing, and numeracy alongside access to networks, tools, and skills. Arthur shares her nontraditional education path, 25 years at the intersection of tech, communications, and education, and how classroom moments like students using Siri pushed her to connect literacy with AI’s growing presence. They discuss integrating AI learning across subjects rather than isolating it, the risk of focusing on tools over outcomes like critical thinking, and concerns about widening divides when basic literacy gaps persist. Arthur describes a shift from an attention economy to an attachment economy in generative AI, the ethics of human–machine relationships, and the need to democratize AI knowledge currently concentrated among a few leaders. She also recounts lobbying that secured $6M for youth skills education and emphasizes community-driven change.

#MBJune26 - Seashell Letters
Seashell Relic
A weathered "B" emerges from a mosaic of shells, starfish, coral fragments, and ocean-worn treasures 
#SeashellArt #Beachcombing #CoastalDecor #OceanInspired #AIart #AIchallenge #aiartcommunity #aiartist #GenAI #GenerativeArt #SynthArt #BuilderofNightmares

images.alt:
A capital letter “B” is constructed entirely from an assemblage of seashells, coral fragments, starfish, and marine debris arranged on a weathered concrete surface. The letter occupies most of the frame and is viewed from directly above. Large spiral shells form the upper curve, while orange conch shells, ridged clam shells, and pale ivory shell fragments create the vertical stem and lower leg. Several small starfish are embedded throughout the composition, their delicate arms extending across the shell-covered surface. Hints of sea sponge, barnacle-encrusted fragments, and textured coral add visual complexity and a sense of age. The background consists of rough gray concrete with patches of peeling paint in faded white and muted red tones, creating a distressed coastal aesthetic. Soft natural light reveals the textures of the shells, highlighting smooth polished surfaces, sandy deposits, chipped edges, and subtle color variations ranging from cream and ivory to coral orange, rust, and pale blue-green. The overall effect resembles a handcrafted typographic sculpture assembled from treasures collected along a shoreline and preserved as a coastal relic.
Keywords:
seashells, letter R, typography, starfish, coral, beachcombing, coastal art, found objects, weathered texture, marine relic

The Cartographer of Flooded Kingdoms
She pulls her weathered cart through a drowned canyon
#DarkFantasy #PostApocalyptic #CinematicArt #EnvironmentalStorytelling #Worldbuilding #AIart #AIchallenge #aiartcommunity #aiartist #GenAI #GenerativeArt #SynthArt #BuilderofNightmares

images.alt:
A lone figure struggles through shallow floodwater in a narrow canyon lined by towering rock walls. Viewed from behind, the traveler wears a heavy, worn cloak trimmed with rough fur and pulls a wooden handcart secured with thick rope. The cart appears aged and waterlogged, its wooden planks darkened by years of exposure. Scattered throughout the flooded landscape are broken posts, beams, and remnants of structures, many leaning at odd angles from the water as though the remains of a long-abandoned settlement. The canyon opens toward a hazy distance where ruined rooftops and skeletal frameworks fade into mist. The color palette is dominated by cold blue-gray and muted teal tones, contrasted by a pale, glowing sky that filters soft light through the fog. Ripples spread across the water’s surface around the traveler’s feet and cart wheels, creating subtle reflections that shimmer beneath the atmospheric haze. The composition draws the eye down the flooded path toward the distant ruins, emphasizing isolation, endurance, and the immense scale of the surrounding landscape. The scene feels cinematic, bleak, and quietly epic, like a survivor crossing the remnants of a drowned world.
Keywords: post-apocalyptic, flooded canyon, lone traveler, wooden cart, ruined settlement, misty landscape, survival, environmental storytelling, cinematic fantasy, blue-gray palette

images.alt:
A monochrome fine art image depicts an elderly woman lying peacefully in bed within a modest bedroom. Her face is softly illuminated from a nearby window, revealing deep lines and closed eyes, suggesting sleep, illness, or the final moments of life. The room is filled with layered translucent forms that resemble drifting smoke, fog, or photographic emulsion. Emerging from this haze is the spectral image of an elderly bearded man whose face appears suspended above the bed. One ghostly arm stretches downward as though reaching toward the woman, yet never quite touches her. Additional indistinct figures and swirling shapes gather around the bedside, blending with shadows and streaks that resemble damaged antique photographic plates. Floral wallpaper, a wardrobe with hanging clothing, and a doorway establish an ordinary domestic setting, while the spectral forms transform it into something uncanny and dreamlike. The image carries the visual character of nineteenth-century spirit photography, with chemical imperfections, blurred apparitions, edge deterioration, and layered exposures creating the impression that memory, grief, dreams, and presence have become inseparable within the same room.
Keywords: spirit photography, apparition, elderly woman, bedroom, ghost, double exposure, monochrome, antique process, smoke, haunting, dreamlike, mortality

National Strawberry Rhubarb Pie Day

images.alt:
On June 9 of each year, National Strawberry Rhubarb Pie Day is observed. Strawberry rhubarb pie is a type of pie that is typically made with a combination of sliced strawberries and chopped rhubarb, which are mixed together with sugar, cornstarch or flour, and sometimes other ingredients such as lemon juice, cinnamon, or vanilla extract.
The filling is then placed in a pie crust and baked until the crust is golden brown and the filling is bubbling.
Strawberry rhubarb pie is a popular dessert in many parts of the world, especially in North America where rhubarb is commonly grown. The combination of the sweet strawberries and tart rhubarb creates a unique and delicious flavor that is beloved by many. It is often served with a scoop of vanilla ice cream or a dollop of whipped cream. Some people also enjoy eating strawberry rhubarb pie for breakfast or brunch.
